\section{EBSD Local Misorientation Foundations}

This note records the initial PyTex definition of local misorientation on regular EBSD grids.

\subsection{Scope}

The current implementation supports:

\begin{itemize}
  \item regular two-dimensional grids with explicit grid shape metadata,
  \item four- and eight-neighbor connectivity,
  \item kernel-average misorientation (KAM) computed as the arithmetic mean of neighbor misorientation angles.
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Definition}

For a measurement site $i$ with valid neighbor set $\mathcal{N}(i)$, PyTex currently defines

\[
\mathrm{KAM}(i) = \frac{1}{|\mathcal{N}(i)|} \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}(i)} \omega(g_i, g_j),
\]

where $\omega(g_i, g_j)$ is the misorientation angle between orientations $g_i$ and $g_j$, optionally reduced by the active crystal symmetry.

\subsection{Current Limits}

\begin{itemize}
  \item No thresholded KAM truncation is applied yet.
  \item No confidence-index masking or phase filtering is implemented yet.
  \item No grain-aware neighborhood filtering is implemented yet.
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Normative And Informative References}

\begin{itemize}
  \item Bunge, H.-J., \emph{Texture Analysis in Materials Science: Mathematical Methods}.
  \item MTEX documentation and public test categories for EBSD neighborhood metrics, as a parity baseline rather than a semantic source of truth.
\end{itemize}
